data.description:
## Summary
`AcliClient.set_relationship(from, to)` emitted `jira link create --out from --in to`, but
ACLI's `--out`/`--in` are INVERTED vs the naive reading: `--out X --in Y --type Blocks`
creates "Y blocks X" (the `--in` issue is the blocker). So every reconciler-written link was
reversed. This stayed hidden because (a) rebar reads links direction-agnostically, (b) most
links were adopted from Jira rather than written by us, and (c) the tests never asserted
absolute direction.

## Reproduction Steps
1. Create Jira issues X, Y. Call `set_relationship(X, Y, "Blocks")`.
2. Observe Jira shows "Y blocks X" (expected "X blocks Y"). (Live-validated 2026-07-16.)
3. Consequence via the applier: `A blocks B` -> Jira "B blocks A" (reversed); `A depends_on B`
   -> after c8ed added the applier swap, Jira "A blocks B" (reversed).

**Expected:** `A blocks B` -> "A blocks B"; `A depends_on B` -> "B blocks A".
**Actual:** both reversed (blocks reversed since outbound link sync shipped; depends_on
reversed by c8ed, which added the correct applier swap but on top of the inverted primitive).

## Root Cause
`src/rebar/_engine/rebar_reconciler/acli_graph.py::set_relationship` maps `from_key`->`--out`,
`to_key`->`--in`, which is backwards. Fix: emit `--out to_key --in from_key` so the link reads
"from blocks to". With c8ed's applier swap + the relation map, BOTH relations then write the
correct direction. Live-validated end-to-end via the differ+applier (blocks->"A blocks B",
depends_on->"B blocks A").

## Why the tests missed it
The live E2E round-trip (`tests/external/test_link_sync_roundtrip_live.py`) checked only that a
Blocks link existed in EITHER direction and that outbound<->inbound AGREE (a consistency check
that holds even when reversed), and never covered `depends_on`. Unit tests stub
`set_relationship`, so they cannot see the flag order.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] `set_relationship(from, to)` emits `--out to --in from`; a unit test pins the flag order.
- [ ] A live E2E test asserts ABSOLUTE direction for BOTH `blocks` and `depends_on`.
- [ ] Live-validated: blocks->"A blocks B", depends_on->"B blocks A" through rebar's code paths.
- [ ] [operator-attested] the ~96 reversed `blocks` links (and any c8ed-reversed depends_on) are repaired in live Jira.
data.title: Outbound Jira links written REVERSED: set_relationship passes ACLI --out/--in backwards
